key_insight: Active learning can dramatically reduce the number of measurements needed to discover sparse governing equations by sampling where model uncertainty is highest.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient system identification for expensive-to-measure dynamical systems, including ODE and PDE settings in science and engineering.
method: Start with SINDy as the sparse equation discovery model.
Use E-SINDy to estimate epistemic uncertainty across candidate samples.
Iteratively choose the most informative regions for ODEs and PDEs instead of sampling uniformly.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates the approach on the Lorenz system across data budgets and noise levels, plus Burgers' and Kuramoto-Sivashinsky PDEs, and reports accurate recovery with far fewer samples than random sampling.
watch_item: Performance depends on uncertainty estimates and may be sensitive to noise, sampling design, and the complexity of the underlying dynamics.
paper_title: How Low Can You Go? Active Learning for Sparse Model Discovery in the Ultra-Low-Data Limit
